steps.title: Overview: Architecture
steps.description: FilterBoxBuilder is a browser-only TypeScript app for designing air purifier enclosures. It has one DOM entry point, a domain layer that turns settings into geometry, preview renderers for Three.js and SVG, and export paths for laser drawings and print kits.

steps.title: Project Shape
steps.description: The app is intentionally small at the runtime boundary: Vite serves `index.html`, Bun runs tests and scripts, and the browser loads `src/app/main.ts`. The README keeps the upstream hardware context next to the generated web app.

steps.title: Bun and Vite Scripts
steps.description: The scripts in this snippet define the normal developer loop: Vite dev server, TypeScript checking, Vite build, Bun tests, and two Boxes.py audit helpers. The app has no backend process in this package.

steps.title: Browser Entry Point
steps.description: The HTML file has one mount node and one module script. That keeps the browser boundary clear: all app state, event handling, rendering, and export behavior starts in `src/app/main.ts`.

steps.title: Workbench Boot State
steps.description: The initial settings and workbench state are decoded from the URL before controls or previews render. This is why shared URLs can recreate both fabrication mode and view state.

steps.title: Domain Join Point
steps.description: The layout function is the main domain handoff. Browser controls give it raw settings, and it returns normalized settings, cut panels, summary numbers, SVG data, and diagnostics used elsewhere.

steps.title: Preview Classes
steps.description: The browser workbench owns two renderer instances. One renders the assembled enclosure; the other renders cut sheets or print sheets. Later tours separate what each renderer receives from how it draws.

steps.title: Export Route
steps.description: Exports stay client-side too. The active settings choose either SVG drawing export or 3MF/static-file export, and the workbench decides which action is visible.

steps.title: Quick Check
steps.text: Where does this app do its main geometry and export work?
answers: In a local Python server started by Vite
In static SVG files checked into the repo
steps.explanation: `index.html` loads `src/app/main.ts` at line 15, and `package.json` only defines Vite/Bun scripts on lines 6-13. There is no backend runtime in this project.

steps.title: Summary
steps.description: The codebase is a single-browser workbench around one domain pipeline. The next tour names the main data structures so the later geometry, print, preview, and export tours have a shared vocabulary.
